segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Exactly.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Mm, so you can see why obesity is growing.
segments.zh: 嗯，所以你能明白为什么肥胖问题越来越严重了。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Let's think about what we're going to include and what will go where.
segments.zh: 我们来想想要包括哪些内容，以及各部分放哪里。然后就能决定需要哪些幻灯片了。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: Then we can decide what slides we need.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: OK, fine.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Good morning everyone.
segments.zh:
大家早上好。今天我们要探讨一项重要的创造性活动——手工编织。在许多国家都发现了古老的针织衣物，这表明编织是一项历史悠久、遍布全球的活动。
当提到“编织”这个词时，我们很可能会想象一位老人——也许是祖母——坐在火炉旁为自己或家人编织衣物。这是一个温馨的画面，但可能会让你觉得编织是过去的活动——事实上，在过去十年中，它曾被预测会从日常生活中消失。因为尽管人类自己缝制和编织衣物已有很长时间，但当工业机器接管后，许多这类手工艺技能开始衰退——主要是因为它们不再代代相传。然而，这一切正在改变，许多国家编织课程的兴趣实际上在上升，越来越多的人正在寻求正式学习这项技能。随着这一趋势，编织设备的销售数据也在增长。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So today we're going to look at an important creative activity and that's hand knitting.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Ancient knitted garments have been found in many different countries, showing that knitting is a global activity with a long history.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When someone says the word 'knitting' we might well picture an elderly person – a grandmother perhaps – sitting by the fire knitting garments for themselves or other members of the family.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's a homely image, but one that may lead you to feel that knitting is an activity of the past – and, indeed, during the previous decade, it was one of the skills that was predicted to vanish from everyday life.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For although humans have sewn and knitted their own clothing for a very long time, many of these craft-based skills went into decline when industrial machines took over – mainly because they were no longer passed down from one generation to another.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, that's all changing and interest in knitting classes in many countries is actually rising, as more and more people are seeking formal instruction in the skill.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: With that trend, we're also seeing an increase in the sales figures for knitting equipment.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So why do people want to be taught to knit at a time when a machine can readily do the job for them?
segments.zh: 那么，为什么在机器可以轻松完成工作的时候，人们还想学习编织呢？答案是，作为一种手工艺，编织对从事者有很多好处。我们来想想可能有哪些好处。过去很多人编织衣物是因为买不起衣服，但今天，如果你经济困难，编织仍然可以帮到你。如果你有几个孩子都需要保暖的冬衣，编织可以省下不少钱。而且，自己编织衣物的成果会很有成就感，尽管入门所需的技能其实很基础，资金投入也很少。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The answer is that knitting, as a handicraft, has numerous benefits for those doing it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Let's consider what some of these might be.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: While many people knitted garments in the past because they couldn't afford to buy clothes, it's still true today that knitting can be helpful if you're experiencing economic hardship.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you have several children who all need warm

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: winter clothes, knitting may save you a lot of money.
segments.zh: 冬衣，编织可以省下不少钱。而且，自己编织衣物的成果会很有成就感，尽管入门所需的技能其实很基础，资金投入也很少。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And the results of knitting your own clothes can be very rewarding, even though the skills you need to get going are really quite basic and the financial outlay is minimal.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But the more significant benefits in today's world are to do with well-being.
segments.zh: 但在当今世界，更重要的好处与幸福感有关。在一个我们每天上网时间估计长达九小时的世界里，用手做一些手工艺的事情会让我们感觉良好。它让我们从技术化、快节奏的生活压力中解脱出来。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In a world where it's estimated that we spend up to nine hours a day online, doing something with our hands that is craft-based makes us feel good.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It releases us from the stress of a technological, fast-paced life.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now, let's look back a bit to early knitting activities.
segments.zh: 现在，我们回顾一下早期的编织活动。事实上，没人确切知道编织最初是什么时候开始的，但考古遗存为我们提供了大量可供思考的信息。编织有趣的一点是，发现的最早衣物表明，大多数成品是圆形的而非平面的。在埃及发现的3、4世纪的物品显示，像袜子和手套这类保暖手脚的东西，是用四到五根针一体织成的。这与今天大多数只需两根针的编织图案大不相同。而且，最初人们使用的针是用木头和其他天然材料（如骨头）手工雕刻的，而今天的针大多由钢或塑料制成，使用时发出特有的咔嗒声。古人用亚麻、大麻、棉和羊毛制成的纱线编织，这些纱线通常对皮肤很粗糙。纺车使人们能够纺出更细的纱线并大量生产，从而让羊毛在编织业中占据主导地位——通常因其保暖性而受到青睐。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In fact, no one really knows when knitting first began, but archaeological remains have disclosed plenty of information for us to think about.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: One of the interesting things about knitting is that the earliest pieces of clothing that have been found suggest that most of the items produced were round rather than flat.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Discoveries from the 3rd and 4th centuries in Egypt show that things like socks and gloves, that were needed to keep hands and feet warm, were knitted in one piece using four or five needles.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: That's very different from most knitting patterns today, which only require two.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: What's more, the very first needles people used were hand carved out of wood and other natural materials, like bone, whereas today's needles are largely made of steel or plastic and make that characteristic clicking sound when someone's using them.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Ancient people knitted using yarns made from linen, hemp, cotton and wool, and these were often very rough on the skin.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The spinning wheel, which allowed people to make finer yarns and produce much greater quantities of them, led to the dominance of wool in the knitting industry – often favoured for its warmth.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Another interesting fact about knitting is that because it was practised in so many parts of the world for so many purposes, regional differences in style developed.
segments.zh: 关于编织的另一个有趣事实是，由于它在世界许多地方被用于多种目的，因此发展出了地区性的风格差异。这种视觉特征使研究人员能够将随时间出土的编织衣物碎片与穿着者来自的地区或从事的工作相匹配。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This visual identity has allowed researchers to match bits of knitted clothing that have been unearthed over time to the region from which the wearer came or the job that he or she did.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As I've mentioned, knitting offered people from poor communities a way of making extra money while doing other tasks.
segments.zh: 正如我提到的，编织为贫困社区的人们提供了一种在做其他事情的同时赚取额外收入的方式。几个世纪以来，似乎男人、女人和孩子都抓住每一个机会编织，例如在放羊、去市场或乘船时。那么，我们接下来……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For many centuries, it seems, men, women and children took every opportunity to knit, for example, while watching over sheep, walking to market or riding in boats.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, let's move on to take a ...
